capability_rows.name: Exact Ahrefs DR Measurement
capability_rows.success_condition: Daily exact DR can be collected and recorded.

capability_rows.name: DR-Driven Daily Planning
capability_rows.success_condition: Daily plan is biased toward lanes that are most likely to improve DR.

capability_rows.name: Public Verification Loop
capability_rows.success_condition: Review-pending and submitted targets are rechecked until public proof exists or they are downgraded.

capability_rows.name: Discovery Supply Engine
capability_rows.success_condition: System discovers at least 100 new worthwhile target roots per day.

capability_rows.name: Optional Accelerator Architecture
capability_rows.success_condition: Paid or unstable research accelerators can improve throughput when available, but the default daily discovery baseline and SaaS product remain stable without them.

capability_rows.name: Editorial Outreach Engine
capability_rows.success_condition: Editorial and email lanes produce repeatable high-trust opportunities and follow-through.

capability_rows.name: Threaded Editorial Follow-Ups
capability_rows.success_condition: Once an editor replies, later follow-ups stay in-thread with explicit reply headers so the interaction reads like a real conversation, not a new cold pitch.

capability_rows.name: Strongest Submission State Memory
capability_rows.success_condition: Later blocked retries cannot erase earlier submit-success evidence; planning keeps the highest-confidence state until public proof or explicit rejection arrives.

capability_rows.name: Live Submit Failure Memory
capability_rows.success_condition: Once a visible-browser live submit proves a form is broken, that host is downgraded from active recovery/editorial queues until the surface changes.

capability_rows.name: External Submit Surface Qualification
capability_rows.success_condition: Third-party structured forms are recognized, filled, and classified separately from generic contact forms, with explicit confirmation required before counting success.

capability_rows.name: Visible Surface Probe
capability_rows.success_condition: Submission-route probes use a visible desktop browser when available, and host-recovery blockers such as SSL or DNS failures are separated from true no-surface outcomes.

capability_rows.name: Probe Auto-Registered Opportunities
capability_rows.success_condition: High-signal browser probe findings automatically register new opportunities so contact forms and auth gates do not get stranded outside the main execution queues.

capability_rows.name: Captcha Surface Triage
capability_rows.success_condition: Structured submit forms blocked by Turnstile or similar challenges are surfaced as deliberate captcha-recovery targets instead of being lost in weak-source noise.

capability_rows.name: Language-Adaptive Submission Copy
capability_rows.success_condition: Submission descriptions, outreach emails, and contact-form intros are localized to the target host language and do not default to English-only copy.

capability_rows.name: Gated Shell Pattern Memory
capability_rows.success_condition: When a repeated host family or page-shell pattern keeps collapsing into paid/auth/no-submit outcomes, the discovery system learns to demote similar variants faster.

capability_rows.name: Durable Recovery Surface
capability_rows.success_condition: Restart recovery preserves DR state, next actions, and productized learnings.

product_claim_policy.rule: Only markets with real downstream evidence should be shown to SaaS users as proven live supply. Planned markets without evidence must be labeled as buildout or watchlist.

product_surfaces_to_sync.label: Dashboard homepage
product_surfaces_to_sync.summary: Show the current capability contract, adoption state, and recent upgrade history on the main dashboard so product and ops do not rely on chat memory.

product_surfaces_to_sync.label: Product detail page
product_surfaces_to_sync.summary: Show the current proven/buildout/watchlist market tiers, the capability fingerprint, and the most important required SaaS actions inside each product view.

product_surfaces_to_sync.label: Ops status
product_surfaces_to_sync.summary: Expose the current capability fingerprint, review-needed state, and recent capability history in the internal operations status surface.

product_surfaces_to_sync.label: Sales and marketing claims
product_surfaces_to_sync.summary: Keep public claims aligned with proven supply depth; never describe buildout lanes as proven live inventory.

product_surfaces_to_sync.label: Localized copy messaging
product_surfaces_to_sync.summary: Explain that submission and outreach copy adapts to the target host language instead of defaulting to English-only copy.

product_surfaces_to_sync.label: Market strategy explainer
product_surfaces_to_sync.summary: Clarify that EN, JA are overweight anchor markets, while other languages are selective buildout lanes.

copy_update_guidance.customer_summary: Describe multilingual discovery honestly: only proven lanes should be pitched as live supply, and buildout/watchlist lanes should be framed as active expansion work.
copy_update_guidance.public_claim_guardrail: Use proven/buildout/watchlist language in customer-facing copy. Do not flatten the system into a generic 'global coverage' claim.
copy_update_guidance.sales_enablement_note: Lead with English / global as the current proven supply. Explain that EN, JA are overweight anchor markets.
copy_update_guidance.localized_copy_note: Target-language adaptive copy is a real quality differentiator and should be called out in product and sales copy.
copy_update_guidance.operator_note: When the capability fingerprint changes, update product copy and sales language only after the required SaaS actions are reviewed.

required_saas_actions.action: Read src/lib/capability-upgrade-feed.json alongside operational-insights.json on every dashboard/product render.
required_saas_actions.why: This is the machine-readable contract that tells SaaS what changed and what still needs UI adoption.

required_saas_actions.action: Show multilingual markets by tier: proven, priority buildout, and watchlist. Do not market buildout lanes as proven supply.
required_saas_actions.why: The discovery system is now evidence-weighted; product claims have to match real supply depth.

required_saas_actions.action: Expose that submission and outreach copy adapts to target language instead of defaulting to English-only messaging.
required_saas_actions.why: This is now a real capability and a trust/quality differentiator for the SaaS product.

required_saas_actions.action: Explain that English and Japanese are overweight anchor markets, while other languages are selective buildout lanes.
required_saas_actions.why: Prevents customers from assuming the engine is evenly distributed across all languages.

team_handoff_summary.one_line: When this fingerprint changes, SaaS should review the required actions and update claims/UI based on the new capability contract.
team_handoff_summary.current_focus: English is the only proven multilingual discovery lane today; Japanese is an anchor buildout lane, and target-language adaptive copy is now a real capability.
